spec_match.rationale: The page now measures the actual scroll anchoring behavior in nested scrollers. It compares overflow-anchor:none with overflow-anchor:auto by recording the same highlighted target paragraph before and after upstream layout shifts, instead of simulating the v139 result with manual scrollTop math.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the Ad injection and Top edge insert controls. The page reported measured target movement, scroll adjustment, and timestamped logs for both the opt-out and browser-anchored scrollers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the feature-index promise of comparing jumpy and stable scroll anchoring behavior, but now frames the comparison as measured overflow-anchor behavior rather than a synthetic CLS score.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces parser support for overflow-anchor:auto/none, the opt-out failure branch, the browser-anchored compensation branch, reset behavior, and a top-of-scroll boundary where neither scroller can compensate.

references.rationale: The concept page now links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5070370113323008 and the CSS Scroll Anchoring spec.

design_system.rationale: The updated controls use shared CSS variables, set text color when button backgrounds change, avoid raw hex colors, and keep the side-by-side measurement layout responsive.

summary: Pre-fix review found a wrong ChromeStatus link, dead mode-toggle buttons, and simulated scroll-jump logs. Post-fix DevTools MCP testing in Chrome 148 at http://localhost:3000/v139/scroll-anchoring-priority-candidate-fix/cls-debugger/ clicked Ad injection and Top edge insert. Ad injection measured 98.0px movement with 0.0px adjustment in the overflow-anchor:none scroller, and 0.0px movement with 98.0px browser adjustment in the overflow-anchor:auto scroller. Top edge insertion measured movement in both scrollers with 0.0px adjustment, surfacing the boundary branch. Console was clean and local page/style requests returned 200.
